methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $1.1B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.6B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Modal is a Series E AI & Software company founded by Erik Bernhardsson and Akshat Bubna, currently valued at $1.1B post-money after raising $111M. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir), all operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods apply to Modal's valuation. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.1B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier for Series E advancement, implies $1.5B. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage companies yields $6.3B, with the peer set ranging from $1.6B (floor) to $250.0B (xAI, outlier). The median peer valuation of $6.3B reflects contributions from Crusoe Energy ($10.0B), Groq ($7.0B), SandboxAQ ($5.6B), and Ayar Labs ($3.8B).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B (low) to $7.2B (high), with a median of $3.9B. The wide range reflects material dispersion within the peer set; xAI's $250B valuation creates substantial upside optionality but is not typical of the cohort. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Modal's product-market fit, revenue traction, and path to unit economics align with higher-performing peers versus the stage-progression baseline. The $1.5B–$3.9B midpoint band may better reflect normalized Series E positioning absent breakthrough capital events.
